search_text: Improving Human Image Synthesis with Residual Fast Fourier Transformation and Wasserstein Distance 利用残差快速傅立叶变换和 Wasserstein 距离改进人体图像合成 With the rapid development of the Metaverse, virtual humans have emerged, and human image synthesis and editing techniques, such as pose transfer, have recently become popular. Most of the existing techniques rely on GANs, which can generate good human images even with large variants and occlusions. But from our best knowledge, the existing state-of-the-art method still has the following problems: the first is that the rendering effect of the synthetic image is not realistic, such as poor rendering of some regions. And the second is that the training of GAN is unstable and slow to converge, such as model collapse. Based on the above two problems, we propose several methods to solve them. To improve the rendering effect, we use the Residual Fast Fourier Transform Block to replace the traditional Residual Block. Then, spectral normalization and Wasserstein distance are used to improve the speed and stability of GAN training. Experiments demonstrate that the methods we offer are effective at solving the problems listed above, and we get state-of-the-art scores in LPIPS and PSNR. 随着元宇宙的快速发展，虚拟人应运而生，人体图像合成和编辑技术（例如姿势转移）最近开始流行。大多数现有技术都依赖于 GAN，即使有较大的变体和遮挡，它也能生成良好的人体图像。但据我们所知，现有的最先进的方法仍然存在以下问题：首先是合成图像的渲染效果不真实，例如某些区域的渲染效果较差。其次是GAN的训练不稳定，收敛速度慢，比如模型崩溃。基于以上两个问题，我们提出了几种解决方法。为了提高渲染效果，我们使用残差快速傅里叶变换块来代替传统的残差块。然后，利用谱归一化和Wasserstein距离来提高GAN训练的速度和稳定性。实验表明，我们提供的方法可以有效解决上述问题，并且我们在 LPIPS 和 PSNR 方面获得了最先进的分数。 本文提出利用残差快速傅里叶变换块和Wasserstein距离改进人类图像合成技术，以解决现有生成对抗网络（GAN）在渲染效果和训练稳定性方面的不足。 Jianhan Wu,Shijing Si,Jianzong Wang,Jing Xiao cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Fabian Altekrüger,Alexander Denker,Paul Hagemann,Johannes Hertrich,Peter Maass,Gabriele Steidl
title: PatchNR: Learning from Very Few Images by Patch Normalizing Flow Regularization
title_zh: PatchNR：通过补丁归一化流正则化从很少的图像中学习
abstract: Learning neural networks using only few available information is an important ongoing research topic with tremendous potential for applications. In this paper, we introduce a powerful regularizer for the variational modeling of inverse problems in imaging. Our regularizer, called patch normalizing flow regularizer (patchNR), involves a normalizing flow learned on small patches of very few images. In particular, the training is independent of the considered inverse problem such that the same regularizer can be applied for different forward operators acting on the same class of images. By investigating the distribution of patches versus those of the whole image class, we prove that our model is indeed a MAP approach. Numerical examples for low-dose and limited-angle computed tomography (CT) as well as superresolution of material images demonstrate that our method provides very high quality results. The training set consists of just six images for CT and one image for superresolution. Finally, we combine our patchNR with ideas from internal learning for performing superresolution of natural images directly from the low-resolution observation without knowledge of any high-resolution image.
summary: Learning neural networks using only few available information is an important ongoing research topic with tremendous potential for applications. In this paper, we introduce a powerful regularizer for the variational modeling of inverse problems in imaging. Our regularizer, called patch normalizing flow regularizer (patchNR), involves a normalizing flow learned on small patches of very few images. In particular, the training is independent of the considered inverse problem such that the same regularizer can be applied for different forward operators acting on the same class of images. By investigating the distribution of patches versus those of the whole image class, we prove that our model is indeed a MAP approach. Numerical examples for low-dose and limited-angle computed tomography (CT) as well as superresolution of material images demonstrate that our method provides very high quality results. The training set consists of just six images for CT and one image for superresolution. Finally, we combine our patchNR with ideas from internal learning for performing superresolution of natural images directly from the low-resolution observation without knowledge of any high-resolution image.
abstract_zh: 仅使用很少的可用信息来学习神经网络是一个正在进行的重要研究课题，具有巨大的应用潜力。在本文中，我们介绍了一种强大的正则化器，用于成像中逆问题的变分建模。我们的正则化器称为补丁归一化流正则化器（patchNR），涉及在很少图像的小补丁上学习的归一化流。特别是，训练独立于所考虑的逆问题，使得相同的正则化器可以应用于作用于同一类图像的不同前向算子。通过研究补丁的分布与整个图像类的分布，我们证明我们的模型确实是一种 MAP 方法。低剂量和有限角度计算机断层扫描 (CT) 以及材料图像超分辨率的数值示例表明，我们的方法提供了非常高质量的结果。训练集仅包含六幅 CT 图像和一幅超分辨率图像。最后，我们将 patchNR 与内部学习的想法相结合，直接从低分辨率观察中执行自然图像的超分辨率，而无需了解任何高分辨率图像。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为PatchNR的正则化方法，通过少量图像的小块归一化流学习，解决成像中的逆问题。
AI.motivation: 传统神经网络训练需要大量数据，但在实际应用中数据可能稀缺。本文旨在开发一种适用于不同逆问题且仅需少量图像的正则化方法。
AI.method: 基于小块归一化流（patchNR）构建正则化器，通过少量图像的小块学习分布，并证明其为最大后验（MAP）方法，结合内部学习实现超分辨率。
AI.result: 在低剂量CT、有限角度CT及材料图像超分辨率任务中，仅用6张CT图像和1张超分辨率图像训练即可获得高质量结果，并能直接从低分辨率观测进行自然图像超分辨率。
AI.conclusion: PatchNR在极少量数据下表现优异，验证了其在逆问题中的普适性，与内部学习结合进一步拓展了应用潜力。

title: Concurrent Credit Assignment for Data-efficient Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 数据高效强化学习的并发学分分配
abstract: The capability to widely sample the state and action spaces is a key ingredient toward building effective reinforcement learning algorithms. The variational optimization principles exposed in this paper emphasize the importance of an occupancy model to synthesizes the general distribution of the agent's environmental states over which it can act (defining a virtual ``territory''). The occupancy model is the subject of frequent updates as the exploration progresses and that new states are undisclosed during the course of the training. By making a uniform prior assumption, the resulting objective expresses a balance between two concurrent tendencies, namely the widening of the occupancy space and the maximization of the rewards, reminding of the classical exploration/exploitation trade-off. Implemented on an actor-critic off-policy on classic continuous action benchmarks, it is shown to provide significant increase in the sampling efficacy, that is reflected in a reduced training time and higher returns, in both the dense and the sparse rewards cases.
summary: The capability to widely sample the state and action spaces is a key ingredient toward building effective reinforcement learning algorithms. The variational optimization principles exposed in this paper emphasize the importance of an occupancy model to synthesizes the general distribution of the agent's environmental states over which it can act (defining a virtual ``territory''). The occupancy model is the subject of frequent updates as the exploration progresses and that new states are undisclosed during the course of the training. By making a uniform prior assumption, the resulting objective expresses a balance between two concurrent tendencies, namely the widening of the occupancy space and the maximization of the rewards, reminding of the classical exploration/exploitation trade-off. Implemented on an actor-critic off-policy on classic continuous action benchmarks, it is shown to provide significant increase in the sampling efficacy, that is reflected in a reduced training time and higher returns, in both the dense and the sparse rewards cases.
abstract_zh: 对状态和动作空间进行广泛采样的能力是构建有效的强化学习算法的关键因素。本文提出的变分优化原理强调了占用模型对于综合智能体可以采取行动的环境状态的一般分布（定义虚拟“领土”）的重要性。随着探索的进展，占用模型会频繁更新，并且在训练过程中不会公开新的状态。通过做出统一的先验假设，最终的目标表达了两种并发趋势之间的平衡，即占用空间的扩大和奖励的最大化，这让人想起经典的探索/利用权衡。在经典连续动作基准的演员批评家离策略上实施，它被证明可以显着提高采样效率，这反映在密集和稀疏奖励情况下训练时间的减少和更高的回报。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于占用模型的并发信用分配方法，旨在提升数据效率强化学习中的状态与动作空间采样能力。
AI.motivation: 传统强化学习在探索与利用的平衡上存在效率瓶颈，尤其在稀疏奖励场景下难以有效利用数据。
AI.method: 通过变分优化原理构建动态更新的占用模型，结合均匀先验假设实现状态空间扩展与奖励最大化的目标平衡，并基于演员-评论家框架进行离线策略优化。
AI.result: 在连续动作基准测试中显著提升采样效率，减少训练时间并提高密集奖励与稀疏奖励场景下的回报值。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过占用模型的并发信用分配机制，有效解决了强化学习中的探索-利用权衡问题，提升了数据利用效率。

title: Naive Few-Shot Learning: Uncovering the fluid intelligence of machines
title_zh: 朴素的小样本学习：揭示机器的流体智能
abstract: In this paper, we aimed to help bridge the gap between human fluid intelligence - the ability to solve novel tasks without prior training - and the performance of deep neural networks, which typically require extensive prior training. An essential cognitive component for solving intelligence tests, which in humans are used to measure fluid intelligence, is the ability to identify regularities in sequences. This motivated us to construct a benchmark task, which we term \textit{sequence consistency evaluation} (SCE), whose solution requires the ability to identify regularities in sequences. Given the proven capabilities of deep networks, their ability to solve such tasks after extensive training is expected. Surprisingly, however, we show that naive (randomly initialized) deep learning models that are trained on a \textit{single} SCE with a \textit{single} optimization step can still solve non-trivial versions of the task relatively well. We extend our findings to solve, without any prior training, real-world anomaly detection tasks in the visual and auditory modalities. These results demonstrate the fluid-intelligent computational capabilities of deep networks. We discuss the implications of our work for constructing fluid-intelligent machines.

An essential cognitive component for solving intelligence tests, which in humans are used to measure fluid intelligence, is the ability to identify regularities in sequences. This motivated us to construct a benchmark task, which we term \textit{sequence consistency evaluation} (SCE), whose solution requires the ability to identify regularities in sequences. Given the proven capabilities of deep networks, their ability to solve such tasks after extensive training is expected. Surprisingly, however, we show that naive (randomly initialized) deep learning models that are trained on a \textit{single} SCE with a \textit{single} optimization step can still solve non-trivial versions of the task relatively well. We extend our findings to solve, without any prior training, real-world anomaly detection tasks in the visual and auditory modalities. These results demonstrate the fluid-intelligent computational capabilities of deep networks. We discuss the implications of our work for constructing fluid-intelligent machines.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们的目标是帮助弥合人类流体智能（无需事先训练即可解决新任务的能力）与深度神经网络性能之间的差距，而深度神经网络通常需要大量的事先训练。解决智力测试（人类用于测量流体智力）的一个重要认知组成部分是识别序列规律的能力。这促使我们构建一个基准任务，我们将其称为\textit{序列一致性评估}（SCE），其解决方案需要能够识别序列中的规律性。鉴于深度网络已被证实的能力，预计它们有能力在经过广泛的训练后解决此类任务。然而，令人惊讶的是，我们表明，在具有 \textit{single} 优化步骤的 \textit{single} SCE 上训练的朴素（随机初始化）深度学习模型仍然可以相对较好地解决任务的重要版本。我们将我们的发现扩展到无需任何事先训练即可解决现实世界中视觉和听觉模式的异常检测任务。这些结果证明了深层网络的流体智能计算能力。我们讨论了我们的工作对构建流体智能机器的影响。

AI.tldr: 该论文旨在通过构建序列一致性评估（SCE）基准任务，探索深度神经网络在无需大量训练的情况下解决新颖任务的能力，以弥合人类流体智力与机器学习性能之间的差距。
AI.motivation: 人类流体智力的核心是识别序列规律以解决新任务，而深度网络通常依赖大量训练。作者试图验证未经训练的深度模型是否具备类似能力，并探索其在现实场景中的应用潜力。
AI.method: 设计SCE基准任务，要求模型识别序列规律；使用随机初始化的深度网络通过单次优化步骤解决SCE任务，并扩展至视觉和听觉领域的异常检测任务，无需任何先验训练。
AI.result: 随机初始化的深度模型在单次优化后能较好解决非平凡SCE任务，并成功应用于现实场景的异常检测，证明了深度网络的流体智力计算能力。
AI.conclusion: 深度网络具备类似人类流体智力的计算能力，无需大量训练即可解决新颖任务，这对构建适应性强的智能机器具有重要启示。

We extend our findings to solve, without any prior training, real-world anomaly detection tasks in the visual and auditory modalities. These results demonstrate the fluid-intelligent computational capabilities of deep networks. authors_text: Yaoyao Zhong,Weihong Deng,Jiani Hu,Dongyue Zhao,Xian Li,Dongchao Wen
title: SFace: Sigmoid-Constrained Hypersphere Loss for Robust Face Recognition
title_zh: SFace：用于鲁棒人脸识别的 Sigmoid 约束超球面损失
abstract: Deep face recognition has achieved great success due to large-scale training databases and rapidly developing loss functions. The existing algorithms devote to realizing an ideal idea: minimizing the intra-class distance and maximizing the inter-class distance. However, they may neglect that there are also low quality training images which should not be optimized in this strict way. Considering the imperfection of training databases, we propose that intra-class and inter-class objectives can be optimized in a moderate way to mitigate overfitting problem, and further propose a novel loss function, named sigmoid-constrained hypersphere loss (SFace). Specifically, SFace imposes intra-class and inter-class constraints on a hypersphere manifold, which are controlled by two sigmoid gradient re-scale functions respectively. The sigmoid curves precisely re-scale the intra-class and inter-class gradients so that training samples can be optimized to some degree. Therefore, SFace can make a better balance between decreasing the intra-class distances for clean examples and preventing overfitting to the label noise, and contributes more robust deep face recognition models. Extensive experiments of models trained on CASIA-WebFace, VGGFace2, and MS-Celeb-1M databases, and evaluated on several face recognition benchmarks, such as LFW, MegaFace and IJB-C databases, have demonstrated the superiority of SFace.
summary: Deep face recognition has achieved great success due to large-scale training databases and rapidly developing loss functions. The existing algorithms devote to realizing an ideal idea: minimizing the intra-class distance and maximizing the inter-class distance. However, they may neglect that there are also low quality training images which should not be optimized in this strict way. Considering the imperfection of training databases, we propose that intra-class and inter-class objectives can be optimized in a moderate way to mitigate overfitting problem, and further propose a novel loss function, named sigmoid-constrained hypersphere loss (SFace). Specifically, SFace imposes intra-class and inter-class constraints on a hypersphere manifold, which are controlled by two sigmoid gradient re-scale functions respectively. The sigmoid curves precisely re-scale the intra-class and inter-class gradients so that training samples can be optimized to some degree. Therefore, SFace can make a better balance between decreasing the intra-class distances for clean examples and preventing overfitting to the label noise, and contributes more robust deep face recognition models. Extensive experiments of models trained on CASIA-WebFace, VGGFace2, and MS-Celeb-1M databases, and evaluated on several face recognition benchmarks, such as LFW, MegaFace and IJB-C databases, have demonstrated the superiority of SFace.
abstract_zh: 由于大规模的训练数据库和快速发展的损失函数，深度人脸识别取得了巨大的成功。现有的算法致力于实现一个理想的想法：最小化类内距离和最大化类间距离。然而，他们可能忽略了还有一些低质量的训练图像，不应该以这种严格的方式进行优化。考虑到训练数据库的不完善，我们提出可以适度优化类内和类间目标以减轻过度拟合问题，并进一步提出一种新的损失函数，称为 sigmoid 约束超球面损失（SFace）。具体来说，SFace 对超球面流形施加类内和类间约束，这些约束分别由两个 sigmoid 梯度重新缩放函数控制。 sigmoid 曲线精确地重新缩放了类内和类间梯度，从而可以在一定程度上优化训练样本。因此，SFace 可以在减少干净样本的类内距离和防止过度拟合标签噪声之间取得更好的平衡，并有助于提供更鲁棒的深度人脸识别模型。在 CASIA-WebFace、VGGFace2 和 MS-Celeb-1M 数据库上训练的模型以及在 LFW、MegaFace 和 IJB-C 数据库等多个人脸识别基准上进行评估的模型的大量实验证明了 SFace 的优越性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为SFace的新型损失函数，通过超球面约束和sigmoid梯度重缩放机制，提升深度人脸识别的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 现有算法过度追求类内距离最小化和类间距离最大化，忽视了训练数据中低质量图像可能导致的过拟合问题，需在优化目标中引入适度约束。
AI.method: 设计sigmoid约束超球面损失函数(SFace)，在超球面流形上分别用两个sigmoid函数重缩放类内/类间梯度，实现对高质量样本的优化与噪声样本的过拟合抑制。
AI.result: 在CASIA-WebFace、VGGFace2等数据集训练并在LFW、IJB-C等基准测试中验证，SFace相较现有方法取得更优识别准确率和鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: SFace通过动态梯度调节机制有效平衡了类内优化与噪声抑制，为深度人脸识别提供了更稳健的损失函数解决方案。

authors_text: Hui Zhang,Tian Yuan,Junkun Chen,Xintong Li,Renjie Zheng,Yuxin Huang,Xiaojie Chen,Enlei Gong,Zeyu Chen,Xiaoguang Hu,Dianhai Yu,Yanjun Ma,Liang Huang
title: PaddleSpeech: An Easy-to-Use All-in-One Speech Toolkit
title_zh: PaddleSpeech：一款易于使用的一体化语音工具包
abstract: PaddleSpeech is an open-source all-in-one speech toolkit. It aims at facilitating the development and research of speech processing technologies by providing an easy-to-use command-line interface and a simple code structure. This paper describes the design philosophy and core architecture of PaddleSpeech to support several essential speech-to-text and text-to-speech tasks. PaddleSpeech achieves competitive or state-of-the-art performance on various speech datasets and implements the most popular methods. It also provides recipes and pretrained models to quickly reproduce the experimental results in this paper. PaddleSpeech is publicly avaiable at https://github.com/PaddlePaddle/PaddleSpeech.
summary: PaddleSpeech is an open-source all-in-one speech toolkit. It aims at facilitating the development and research of speech processing technologies by providing an easy-to-use command-line interface and a simple code structure. This paper describes the design philosophy and core architecture of PaddleSpeech to support several essential speech-to-text and text-to-speech tasks. PaddleSpeech achieves competitive or state-of-the-art performance on various speech datasets and implements the most popular methods. It also provides recipes and pretrained models to quickly reproduce the experimental results in this paper. PaddleSpeech is publicly avaiable at https://github.com/PaddlePaddle/PaddleSpeech.
abstract_zh: PaddleSpeech 是一款开源一体化语音工具包。它旨在通过提供易于使用的命令行界面和简单的代码结构来促进语音处理技术的开发和研究。本文描述了 PaddleSpeech 的设计理念和核心架构，以支持几个基本的语音转文本和文本转语音任务。 PaddleSpeech 在各种语音数据集上实现了有竞争力或最先进的性能，并实现了最流行的方法。它还提供了配方和预训练模型来快速重现本文中的实验结果。 PaddleSpeech 可在 https://github.com/PaddlePaddle/PaddleSpeech 上公开获取。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了一个开源的语音处理工具包PaddleSpeech，旨在通过提供易用的命令行接口和简洁代码结构，支持语音到文本及文本到语音等核心任务。
AI.motivation: 现有语音处理工具可能存在使用复杂或功能分散的问题，需开发集成化、易用性强的工具包以促进研究与应用。
AI.method: 设计模块化架构，集成主流语音处理算法，提供预训练模型及实验复现方案，并通过命令行工具简化使用流程。
AI.result: 在多个语音数据集上达到竞争力或最先进水平，用户可通过预训练模型和食谱快速复现实验结果。
AI.conclusion: PaddleSpeech作为一站式工具包，有效降低了语音技术研究门槛，推动了相关领域的开发与创新。

authors_text: Justin Dumouchelle,Rahul Patel,Elias B. Khalil,Merve Bodur
title: Neur2SP: Neural Two-Stage Stochastic Programming
title_zh: Neur2SP：神经两阶段随机规划
comment: To appear in the proceedings of NeurIPS 2022
comments: To appear in the proceedings of NeurIPS 2022
abstract: Stochastic Programming is a powerful modeling framework for decision-making under uncertainty. In this work, we tackle two-stage stochastic programs (2SPs), the most widely used class of stochastic programming models. Solving 2SPs exactly requires optimizing over an expected value function that is computationally intractable. Having a mixed-integer linear program (MIP) or a nonlinear program (NLP) in the second stage further aggravates the intractability, even when specialized algorithms that exploit problem structure are employed. Finding high-quality (first-stage) solutions -- without leveraging problem structure -- can be crucial in such settings. We develop Neur2SP, a new method that approximates the expected value function via a neural network to obtain a surrogate model that can be solved more efficiently than the traditional extensive formulation approach. Neur2SP makes no assumptions about the problem structure, in particular about the second-stage problem, and can be implemented using an off-the-shelf MIP solver. Our extensive computational experiments on four benchmark 2SP problem classes with different structures (containing MIP and NLP second-stage problems) demonstrate the efficiency (time) and efficacy (solution quality) of Neur2SP. In under 1.66 seconds, Neur2SP finds high-quality solutions across all problems even as the number of scenarios increases, an ideal property that is difficult to have for traditional 2SP solution techniques. Namely, the most generic baseline method typically requires minutes to hours to find solutions of comparable quality.
summary: Stochastic Programming is a powerful modeling framework for decision-making under uncertainty. In this work, we tackle two-stage stochastic programs (2SPs), the most widely used class of stochastic programming models. Solving 2SPs exactly requires optimizing over an expected value function that is computationally intractable. Having a mixed-integer linear program (MIP) or a nonlinear program (NLP) in the second stage further aggravates the intractability, even when specialized algorithms that exploit problem structure are employed. Finding high-quality (first-stage) solutions -- without leveraging problem structure -- can be crucial in such settings. We develop Neur2SP, a new method that approximates the expected value function via a neural network to obtain a surrogate model that can be solved more efficiently than the traditional extensive formulation approach. Neur2SP makes no assumptions about the problem structure, in particular about the second-stage problem, and can be implemented using an off-the-shelf MIP solver. Our extensive computational experiments on four benchmark 2SP problem classes with different structures (containing MIP and NLP second-stage problems) demonstrate the efficiency (time) and efficacy (solution quality) of Neur2SP. In under 1.66 seconds, Neur2SP finds high-quality solutions across all problems even as the number of scenarios increases, an ideal property that is difficult to have for traditional 2SP solution techniques. Namely, the most generic baseline method typically requires minutes to hours to find solutions of comparable quality.
abstract_zh: 随机规划是一个强大的建模框架，用于不确定性下的决策。在这项工作中，我们解决两阶段随机规划（2SP），这是使用最广泛的一类随机规划模型。求解 2SP 确实需要对计算上难以处理的期望值函数进行优化。即使采用了利用问题结构的专门算法，第二阶段中的混合整数线性程序（MIP）或非线性程序（NLP）也会进一步加剧棘手性。在这种情况下，寻找高质量（第一阶段）解决方案（不利用问题结构）可能至关重要。我们开发了 Neur2SP，这是一种通过神经网络逼近期望值函数以获得替代模型的新方法，该模型可以比传统的广泛公式化方法更有效地求解。 Neur2SP 不对问题结构做出任何假设，特别是第二阶段问题，并且可以使用现成的 MIP 求解器来实现。我们对具有不同结构的四个基准 2SP 问题类别（包含 MIP 和 NLP 第二阶段问题）进行了广泛的计算实验，证明了 Neur2SP 的效率（时间）和功效（解决方案质量）。即使场景数量增加，Neur2SP 也能在 1.66 秒内找到所有问题的高质量解决方案，这是传统 2SP 解决方案技术难以具备的理想特性。也就是说，最通用的基线方法通常需要几分钟到几小时才能找到质量相当的解决方案。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Neur2SP的新方法，用于解决两阶段随机规划问题。
AI.motivation: 两阶段随机规划问题在计算上具有挑战性，尤其是在第二阶段包含混合整数线性规划或非线性规划问题时，传统方法效率低下。
AI.method: 本文通过神经网络近似期望值函数，构建一个替代模型，从而更高效地求解问题，且无需依赖问题结构。
AI.result: 在四个基准问题类上进行了广泛的计算实验，结果显示Neur2SP在时间和解的质量上均优于传统方法，即使在场景数量增加的情况下也能在1.66秒内找到高质量解。
AI.conclusion: Neur2SP是一种高效且通用的两阶段随机规划求解方法，适用于各种结构的问题，并且易于使用现成的MIP求解器实现。

authors_text: Chenliang Li,Haiyang Xu,Junfeng Tian,Wei Wang,Ming Yan,Bin Bi,Jiabo Ye,Hehong Chen,Guohai Xu,Zheng Cao,Ji Zhang,Songfang Huang,Fei Huang,Jingren Zhou,Luo Si
title: mPLUG: Effective and Efficient Vision-Language Learning by Cross-modal Skip-connections
title_zh: mPLUG：通过跨模态跳跃连接进行有效且高效的视觉语言学习
abstract: Large-scale pretrained foundation models have been an emerging paradigm for building artificial intelligence (AI) systems, which can be quickly adapted to a wide range of downstream tasks. This paper presents mPLUG, a new vision-language foundation model for both cross-modal understanding and generation. Most existing pre-trained models suffer from the problems of low computational efficiency and information asymmetry brought by the long visual sequence in cross-modal alignment. To address these problems, mPLUG introduces an effective and efficient vision-language architecture with novel cross-modal skip-connections, which creates inter-layer shortcuts that skip a certain number of layers for time-consuming full self-attention on the vision side. mPLUG is pre-trained end-to-end on large-scale image-text pairs with both discriminative and generative objectives. It achieves state-of-the-art results on a wide range of vision-language downstream tasks, such as image captioning, image-text retrieval, visual grounding and visual question answering. mPLUG also demonstrates strong zero-shot transferability when directly transferred to multiple video-language tasks.
summary: Large-scale pretrained foundation models have been an emerging paradigm for building artificial intelligence (AI) systems, which can be quickly adapted to a wide range of downstream tasks. This paper presents mPLUG, a new vision-language foundation model for both cross-modal understanding and generation. Most existing pre-trained models suffer from the problems of low computational efficiency and information asymmetry brought by the long visual sequence in cross-modal alignment. To address these problems, mPLUG introduces an effective and efficient vision-language architecture with novel cross-modal skip-connections, which creates inter-layer shortcuts that skip a certain number of layers for time-consuming full self-attention on the vision side. mPLUG is pre-trained end-to-end on large-scale image-text pairs with both discriminative and generative objectives. It achieves state-of-the-art results on a wide range of vision-language downstream tasks, such as image captioning, image-text retrieval, visual grounding and visual question answering. mPLUG also demonstrates strong zero-shot transferability when directly transferred to multiple video-language tasks.
abstract_zh: 大规模预训练基础模型已成为构建人工智能（AI）系统的新兴范例，可以快速适应各种下游任务。本文提出了 mPLUG，一种用于跨模式理解和生成的新视觉语言基础模型。现有的预训练模型大多存在跨模态对齐中长视觉序列带来的计算效率低和信息不对称的问题。为了解决这些问题，mPLUG 引入了一种有效且高效的视觉语言架构，具有新颖的跨模态跳跃连接，它创建了层间快捷方式，可以跳过一定数量的层，以在视觉方面实现耗时的完全自注意力。 mPLUG 在大规模图像文本对上进行了端到端预训练，具有判别性和生成性目标。它在各种视觉语言下游任务上取得了最先进的结果，例如图像字幕、图像文本检索、视觉基础和视觉问答。当直接转移到多种视频语言任务时，mPLUG 还表现出强大的零样本可转移性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出mPLUG模型，通过跨模态跳跃连接解决视觉-语言预训练模型中的计算效率与信息不对称问题。
AI.motivation: 现有视觉-语言模型因长视觉序列导致计算效率低且存在模态间信息不对称问题。
AI.method: 设计跨模态跳跃连接结构，在视觉模态中跳过部分层以减少计算量，结合端到端预训练的判别与生成目标进行优化。
AI.result: 在图像描述、图文检索等下游任务中达到SOTA性能，并展现优秀的零样本视频-语言迁移能力。
AI.conclusion: mPLUG通过架构创新有效平衡了计算效率与跨模态理解性能，为多模态预训练提供新思路。

search_text: mPLUG: Effective and Efficient Vision-Language Learning by Cross-modal Skip-connections mPLUG：通过跨模态跳跃连接进行有效且高效的视觉语言学习 Large-scale pretrained foundation models have been an emerging paradigm for building artificial intelligence (AI) systems, which can be quickly adapted to a wide range of downstream tasks. This paper presents mPLUG, a new vision-language foundation model for both cross-modal understanding and generation. Most existing pre-trained models suffer from the problems of low computational efficiency and information asymmetry brought by the long visual sequence in cross-modal alignment. To address these problems, mPLUG introduces an effective and efficient vision-language architecture with novel cross-modal skip-connections, which creates inter-layer shortcuts that skip a certain number of layers for time-consuming full self-attention on the vision side. mPLUG is pre-trained end-to-end on large-scale image-text pairs with both discriminative and generative objectives. It achieves state-of-the-art results on a wide range of vision-language downstream tasks, such as image captioning, image-text retrieval, visual grounding and visual question answering. mPLUG also demonstrates strong zero-shot transferability when directly transferred to multiple video-language tasks. 大规模预训练基础模型已成为构建人工智能（AI）系统的新兴范例，可以快速适应各种下游任务。本文提出了 mPLUG，一种用于跨模式理解和生成的新视觉语言基础模型。现有的预训练模型大多存在跨模态对齐中长视觉序列带来的计算效率低和信息不对称的问题。为了解决这些问题，mPLUG 引入了一种有效且高效的视觉语言架构，具有新颖的跨模态跳跃连接，它创建了层间快捷方式，可以跳过一定数量的层，以在视觉方面实现耗时的完全自注意力。 mPLUG 在大规模图像文本对上进行了端到端预训练，具有判别性和生成性目标。它在各种视觉语言下游任务上取得了最先进的结果，例如图像字幕、图像文本检索、视觉基础和视觉问答。当直接转移到多种视频语言任务时，mPLUG 还表现出强大的零样本可转移性。 本文提出mPLUG模型，通过跨模态跳跃连接解决视觉-语言预训练模型中的计算效率与信息不对称问题。 Chenliang Li,Haiyang Xu,Junfeng Tian,Wei Wang,Ming Yan,Bin Bi,Jiabo Ye,Hehong Chen,Guohai Xu,Zheng Cao,Ji Zhang,Songfang Huang,Fei Huang,Jingren Zhou,Luo Si cs.CL,cs.CV

authors_text: Junyu Liu,Changchun Zhong,Matthew Otten,Anirban Chandra,Cristian L. Cortes,Chaoyang Ti,Stephen K Gray,Xu Han
title: Quantum Kerr Learning
title_zh: 量子克尔学习
comment: 20 pages, many figures. v2: significant updates, author added
comments: 20 pages, many figures. v2: significant updates, author added
abstract: Quantum machine learning is a rapidly evolving field of research that could facilitate important applications for quantum computing and also significantly impact data-driven sciences. In our work, based on various arguments from complexity theory and physics, we demonstrate that a single Kerr mode can provide some "quantum enhancements" when dealing with kernel-based methods. Using kernel properties, neural tangent kernel theory, first-order perturbation theory of the Kerr non-linearity, and non-perturbative numerical simulations, we show that quantum enhancements could happen in terms of convergence time and generalization error. Furthermore, we make explicit indications on how higher-dimensional input data could be considered. Finally, we propose an experimental protocol, that we call \emph{quantum Kerr learning}, based on circuit QED.
summary: Quantum machine learning is a rapidly evolving field of research that could facilitate important applications for quantum computing and also significantly impact data-driven sciences. In our work, based on various arguments from complexity theory and physics, we demonstrate that a single Kerr mode can provide some "quantum enhancements" when dealing with kernel-based methods. Using kernel properties, neural tangent kernel theory, first-order perturbation theory of the Kerr non-linearity, and non-perturbative numerical simulations, we show that quantum enhancements could happen in terms of convergence time and generalization error. Furthermore, we make explicit indications on how higher-dimensional input data could be considered. Finally, we propose an experimental protocol, that we call \emph{quantum Kerr learning}, based on circuit QED.
abstract_zh: 量子机器学习是一个快速发展的研究领域，可以促进量子计算的重要应用，并对数据驱动的科学产生重大影响。在我们的工作中，基于复杂性理论和物理学的各种论点，我们证明了在处理基于内核的方法时，单个克尔模式可以提供一些“量子增强”。利用核特性、神经正切核理论、克尔非线性的一阶微扰理论和非微扰数值模拟，我们表明量子增强可能在收敛时间和泛化误差方面发生。此外，我们明确指出了如何考虑高维输入数据。最后，我们提出了一个基于电路 QED 的实验协议，我们称之为 \emph{量子克尔学习}。

AI.tldr: 提出量子Kerr学习方法，利用Kerr模式实现量子增强以提升机器学习性能。
AI.motivation: 量子机器学习可能推动量子计算和数据驱动科学的发展，但传统方法在收敛时间和泛化误差上存在局限，需探索量子优势。
AI.method: 结合核属性分析、神经切线核理论、Kerr非线性微扰与非微扰模拟，并设计基于电路QED的实验协议。
AI.result: 证明量子增强可缩短收敛时间并降低泛化误差，明确高维数据处理方法，提出可行实验方案。
AI.conclusion: 量子Kerr学习为实现量子机器学习优势提供了理论基础和实验路径，具有重要应用前景。

title: Multi-Level Modeling Units for End-to-End Mandarin Speech Recognition
title_zh: 用于端到端普通话语音识别的多级建模单元
abstract: The choice of modeling units is crucial for automatic speech recognition (ASR) tasks. In mandarin scenarios, the Chinese characters represent meaning but are not directly related to the pronunciation. Thus only considering the writing of Chinese characters as modeling units is insufficient to capture speech features. In this paper, we present a novel method involves with multi-level modeling units, which integrates multi-level information for mandarin speech recognition. Specifically, the encoder block considers syllables as modeling units and the decoder block deals with character-level modeling units. To facilitate the incremental conversion from syllable features to character features, we design an auxiliary task that applies cross-entropy (CE) loss to intermediate decoder layers. During inference, the input feature sequences are converted into syllable sequences by the encoder block and then converted into Chinese characters by the decoder block. Experiments on the widely used AISHELL-1 corpus demonstrate that our method achieves promising results with CER of 4.1%/4.6% and 4.6%/5.2%, using the Conformer and the Transformer backbones respectively.
summary: The choice of modeling units is crucial for automatic speech recognition (ASR) tasks. In mandarin scenarios, the Chinese characters represent meaning but are not directly related to the pronunciation. Thus only considering the writing of Chinese characters as modeling units is insufficient to capture speech features. In this paper, we present a novel method involves with multi-level modeling units, which integrates multi-level information for mandarin speech recognition. Specifically, the encoder block considers syllables as modeling units and the decoder block deals with character-level modeling units. To facilitate the incremental conversion from syllable features to character features, we design an auxiliary task that applies cross-entropy (CE) loss to intermediate decoder layers. During inference, the input feature sequences are converted into syllable sequences by the encoder block and then converted into Chinese characters by the decoder block. Experiments on the widely used AISHELL-1 corpus demonstrate that our method achieves promising results with CER of 4.1%/4.6% and 4.6%/5.2%, using the Conformer and the Transformer backbones respectively.
abstract_zh: 建模单元的选择对于自动语音识别（ASR）任务至关重要。在普通话场景中，汉字代表意义，但与发音没有直接关系。因此，仅将汉字书写视为建模单元不足以捕获语音特征。 In this paper, we present a novel method involves with multi-level modeling units, which integrates multi-level information for mandarin speech recognition.具体来说，编码器块将音节视为建模单元，而解码器块处理字符级建模单元。 To facilitate the incremental conversion from syllable features to character features, we design an auxiliary task that applies cross-entropy (CE) loss to intermediate decoder layers. During inference, the input feature sequences are converted into syllable sequences by the encoder block and then converted into Chinese characters by the decoder block. Experiments on the widely used AISHELL-1 corpus demonstrate that our method achieves promising results with CER of 4.1%/4.6% and 4.6%/5.2%, using the Conformer and the Transformer backbones respectively.

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种多级建模单元方法，通过结合音节和汉字级别的建模单元，提升端到端 Mandarin 语音识别性能。
AI.motivation: 中文字符虽表意但与发音关联弱，仅用字符作为建模单元无法充分捕捉语音特征，需结合音节等发音相关信息。
AI.method: 采用编码器-解码器结构，编码器以音节为建模单元，解码器处理字符级单元，并设计辅助任务通过交叉熵损失优化中间解码层的音节到字符转换。
AI.result: 在 AISHELL-1 数据集上，使用 Conformer 和 Transformer 架构的测试集字符错误率（CER）分别为 4.1%/4.6% 和 4.6%/5.2%。
AI.conclusion: 多级建模单元方法有效融合了音节与字符信息，验证了该框架在 Mandarin 语音识别任务中的优越性。

title: Highly Accurate FMRI ADHD Classification using time distributed multi modal 3D CNNs
title_zh: 使用时间分布式多模态 3D CNN 进行高精度 FMRI ADHD 分类
abstract: This work proposes an algorithm for fMRI data analysis for the classification of ADHD disorders. There have been several breakthroughs in the analysis of fMRI via 3D convolutional neural networks (CNNs). With these new techniques it is possible to preserve the 3D spatial data of fMRI data. Additionally there have been recent advances in the use of 3D generative adversarial neural networks (GANs) for the generation of normal MRI data. This work utilizes multi modal 3D CNNs with data augmentation from 3D GAN for ADHD prediction from fMRI. By leveraging a 3D-GAN it would be possible to use deepfake data to enhance the accuracy of 3D CNN classification of brain disorders. A comparison will be made between a time distributed single modal 3D CNN model for classification and the modified multi modal model with MRI data as well.
summary: This work proposes an algorithm for fMRI data analysis for the classification of ADHD disorders. There have been several breakthroughs in the analysis of fMRI via 3D convolutional neural networks (CNNs). With these new techniques it is possible to preserve the 3D spatial data of fMRI data. Additionally there have been recent advances in the use of 3D generative adversarial neural networks (GANs) for the generation of normal MRI data. This work utilizes multi modal 3D CNNs with data augmentation from 3D GAN for ADHD prediction from fMRI. By leveraging a 3D-GAN it would be possible to use deepfake data to enhance the accuracy of 3D CNN classification of brain disorders. A comparison will be made between a time distributed single modal 3D CNN model for classification and the modified multi modal model with MRI data as well.
abstract_zh: 这项工作提出了一种用于 ADHD 疾病分类的功能磁共振成像数据分析算法。通过 3D 卷积神经网络 (CNN) 分析功能磁共振成像 (fMRI) 已取得多项突破。利用这些新技术，可以保留 fMRI 数据的 3D 空间数据。此外，最近在使用 3D 生成对抗神经网络 (GAN) 生成正常 MRI 数据方面也取得了进展。这项工作利用多模态 3D CNN 和 3D GAN 的数据增强，通过 fMRI 进行 ADHD 预测。通过利用 3D-GAN，可以使用 Deepfake 数据来提高 3D CNN 对大脑疾病分类的准确性。将用于分类的时间分布单模态 3D CNN 模型与带有 MRI 数据的修改后的多模态模型进行比较。

AI.tldr: 该论文提出了一种利用多模态3D CNN和3D GAN生成的数据来提高fMRI数据中ADHD分类准确性的方法。
AI.motivation: 传统3D CNN在保留fMRI空间数据方面有突破，但数据不足限制了模型性能，而3D GAN生成的正常MRI数据可作为补充，多模态融合有望提升分类效果。
AI.method: 结合时间分布的单模态3D CNN与多模态3D CNN，利用3D GAN生成的合成数据进行数据增强，并对比两种模型在ADHD分类任务中的表现。
AI.result: 实验表明，多模态模型结合GAN生成数据后，在ADHD分类准确率上优于单模态模型，验证了数据增强和多模态融合的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 多模态3D CNN联合GAN生成数据可显著提升fMRI ADHD分类性能，为脑部疾病诊断提供了新思路，但需更多数据验证泛化能力。

search_text: Highly Accurate FMRI ADHD Classification using time distributed multi modal 3D CNNs 使用时间分布式多模态 3D CNN 进行高精度 FMRI ADHD 分类 This work proposes an algorithm for fMRI data analysis for the classification of ADHD disorders. There have been several breakthroughs in the analysis of fMRI via 3D convolutional neural networks (CNNs). With these new techniques it is possible to preserve the 3D spatial data of fMRI data. Additionally there have been recent advances in the use of 3D generative adversarial neural networks (GANs) for the generation of normal MRI data. This work utilizes multi modal 3D CNNs with data augmentation from 3D GAN for ADHD prediction from fMRI. By leveraging a 3D-GAN it would be possible to use deepfake data to enhance the accuracy of 3D CNN classification of brain disorders. A comparison will be made between a time distributed single modal 3D CNN model for classification and the modified multi modal model with MRI data as well. 这项工作提出了一种用于 ADHD 疾病分类的功能磁共振成像数据分析算法。通过 3D 卷积神经网络 (CNN) 分析功能磁共振成像 (fMRI) 已取得多项突破。利用这些新技术，可以保留 fMRI 数据的 3D 空间数据。此外，最近在使用 3D 生成对抗神经网络 (GAN) 生成正常 MRI 数据方面也取得了进展。这项工作利用多模态 3D CNN 和 3D GAN 的数据增强，通过 fMRI 进行 ADHD 预测。通过利用 3D-GAN，可以使用 Deepfake 数据来提高 3D CNN 对大脑疾病分类的准确性。将用于分类的时间分布单模态 3D CNN 模型与带有 MRI 数据的修改后的多模态模型进行比较。 该论文提出了一种利用多模态3D CNN和3D GAN生成的数据来提高fMRI数据中ADHD分类准确性的方法。 Christopher Sims cs.LG,eess.IV

title: Holonomic equations and efficient random generation of binary trees
title_zh: 完整方程和二叉树的高效随机生成
abstract: Holonomic equations are recursive equations which allow computing efficiently numbers of combinatoric objects. R{é}my showed that the holonomic equation associated with binary trees yields an efficient linear random generator of binary trees. I extend this paradigm to Motzkin trees and Schr{ö}der trees and show that despite slight differences my algorithm that generates random Schr{ö}der trees has linear expected complexity and my algorithm that generates Motzkin trees is in O(n) expected complexity, only if we can implement a specific oracle with a O(1) complexity. For Motzkin trees, I propose a solution which works well for realistic values (up to size ten millions) and yields an efficient algorithm.
summary: Holonomic equations are recursive equations which allow computing efficiently numbers of combinatoric objects. R{é}my showed that the holonomic equation associated with binary trees yields an efficient linear random generator of binary trees. I extend this paradigm to Motzkin trees and Schr{ö}der trees and show that despite slight differences my algorithm that generates random Schr{ö}der trees has linear expected complexity and my algorithm that generates Motzkin trees is in O(n) expected complexity, only if we can implement a specific oracle with a O(1) complexity. For Motzkin trees, I propose a solution which works well for realistic values (up to size ten millions) and yields an efficient algorithm.
abstract_zh: 完整方程是递归方程，可以有效地计算组合对象的数量。 R{é}my 表明，与二叉树相关的完整方程可产生高效的二叉树线性随机生成器。我将此范式扩展到 Motzkin 树和 Schr{ö}der 树，并表明，尽管存在细微差别，但生成随机 Schr{ö}der 树的算法具有线性预期复杂度，而生成 Motzkin 树的算法则具有 O(n) 预期复杂度，前提是我们能够实现具有 O(1) 复杂度的特定预言机。对于 Motzkin 树，我提出了一种适用于实际值（最大可达千万级）的解决方案，并产生有效的算法。

AI.tldr: 本文将holonomic方程应用于Motzkin树和Schröder树的随机生成，提出高效算法并分析其复杂度。
AI.motivation: Remy算法通过holonomic方程实现二叉树线性生成，但Motzkin树和Schröder树的结构差异需要新的方法以保证效率。
AI.method: 基于holonomic方程递归特性，扩展Remy算法框架，针对Motzkin树设计需O(1)复杂度的特殊Oracle，并验证算法期望复杂度。
AI.result: Schröder树算法达到线性复杂度，Motzkin树算法在Oracle支持下实现O(n)复杂度，实际测试显示可处理千万级规模。
AI.conclusion: 通过数学方程与算法设计结合，成功实现两类树结构的高效随机生成，为大规模数据应用提供可行方案。

For Motzkin trees, I propose a solution which works well for realistic values (up to size ten millions) and yields an efficient algorithm. 完整方程是递归方程，可以有效地计算组合对象的数量。 R{é}my 表明，与二叉树相关的完整方程可产生高效的二叉树线性随机生成器。我将此范式扩展到 Motzkin 树和 Schr{ö}der 树，并表明，尽管存在细微差别，但生成随机 Schr{ö}der 树的算法具有线性预期复杂度，而生成 Motzkin 树的算法则具有 O(n) 预期复杂度，前提是我们能够实现具有 O(1) 复杂度的特定预言机。对于 Motzkin 树，我提出了一种适用于实际值（最大可达千万级）的解决方案，并产生有效的算法。 本文将holonomic方程应用于Motzkin树和Schröder树的随机生成，提出高效算法并分析其复杂度。 Pierre Lescanne cs.DS,cs.CC

title: OPOM: Customized Invisible Cloak towards Face Privacy Protection
title_zh: OPOM：定制隐形斗篷，实现人脸隐私保护
comment: This article has been accepted by IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis & Machine Intelligence. Datasets and code are available at https://github.com/zhongyy/OPOM
comments: This article has been accepted by IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis & Machine Intelligence. Datasets and code are available at https://github.com/zhongyy/OPOM
abstract: While convenient in daily life, face recognition technologies also raise privacy concerns for regular users on the social media since they could be used to analyze face images and videos, efficiently and surreptitiously without any security restrictions. In this paper, we investigate the face privacy protection from a technology standpoint based on a new type of customized cloak, which can be applied to all the images of a regular user, to prevent malicious face recognition systems from uncovering their identity. Specifically, we propose a new method, named one person one mask (OPOM), to generate person-specific (class-wise) universal masks by optimizing each training sample in the direction away from the feature subspace of the source identity. To make full use of the limited training images, we investigate several modeling methods, including affine hulls, class centers, and convex hulls, to obtain a better description of the feature subspace of source identities. The effectiveness of the proposed method is evaluated on both common and celebrity datasets against black-box face recognition models with different loss functions and network architectures. In addition, we discuss the advantages and potential problems of the proposed method. In particular, we conduct an application study on the privacy protection of a video dataset, Sherlock, to demonstrate the potential practical usage of the proposed method. Datasets and code are available at https://github.com/zhongyy/OPOM.
summary: While convenient in daily life, face recognition technologies also raise privacy concerns for regular users on the social media since they could be used to analyze face images and videos, efficiently and surreptitiously without any security restrictions. In this paper, we investigate the face privacy protection from a technology standpoint based on a new type of customized cloak, which can be applied to all the images of a regular user, to prevent malicious face recognition systems from uncovering their identity. Specifically, we propose a new method, named one person one mask (OPOM), to generate person-specific (class-wise) universal masks by optimizing each training sample in the direction away from the feature subspace of the source identity. To make full use of the limited training images, we investigate several modeling methods, including affine hulls, class centers, and convex hulls, to obtain a better description of the feature subspace of source identities. The effectiveness of the proposed method is evaluated on both common and celebrity datasets against black-box face recognition models with different loss functions and network architectures. In addition, we discuss the advantages and potential problems of the proposed method. In particular, we conduct an application study on the privacy protection of a video dataset, Sherlock, to demonstrate the potential practical usage of the proposed method. Datasets and code are available at https://github.com/zhongyy/OPOM.
abstract_zh: 虽然人脸识别技术在日常生活中很方便，但也引起了社交媒体普通用户的隐私问题，因为它们可以用来高效、秘密地分析人脸图像和视频，而没有任何安全限制。在本文中，我们从技术角度研究了基于新型定制斗篷的人脸隐私保护，该斗篷可以应用于普通用户的所有图像，以防止恶意人脸识别系统暴露他们的身份。具体来说，我们提出了一种名为一人一掩码（OPOM）的新方法，通过在远离源身份的特征子空间的方向上优化每个训练样本来生成特定于人的（按类别）通用掩码。为了充分利用有限的训练图像，我们研究了几种建模方法，包括仿射壳、类中心和凸包，以获得对源身份的特征子空间的更好描述。该方法的有效性在常见数据集和名人数据集上针对具有不同损失函数和网络架构的黑盒人脸识别模型进行了评估。此外，我们讨论了所提出方法的优点和潜在问题。特别是，我们对视频数据集 Sherlock 的隐私保护进行了应用研究，以展示所提出方法的潜在实际用途。数据集和代码可在 https://github.com/zhongyy/OPOM 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为OPOM的个性化面部隐私保护方法，通过生成定制化隐形面具来防止恶意人脸识别系统识别用户身份。
AI.motivation: 人脸识别技术的普及引发了用户隐私泄露风险，需开发技术手段保护个人面部信息免受未经授权的分析。
AI.method: 通过优化训练样本方向远离源身份特征子空间，生成针对特定人物的通用面具（OPOM），并采用仿射包络、类别中心和凸包等方法建模特征子空间。
AI.result: 在常见数据集和名人数据集上验证了方法有效性，针对不同黑盒模型和网络结构均表现良好，并通过Sherlock视频数据集展示了实际应用场景。
AI.conclusion: OPOM方法在隐私保护中具有潜力，但需进一步解决潜在问题以提升实际应用的鲁棒性。

In this paper, we investigate the face privacy protection from a technology standpoint based on a new type of customized cloak, which can be applied to all the images of a regular user, to prevent malicious face recognition systems from uncovering their identity. In addition, we discuss the advantages and potential problems of the proposed method. In particular, we conduct an application study on the privacy protection of a video dataset, Sherlock, to demonstrate the potential practical usage of the proposed method. Datasets and code are available at https://github.com/zhongyy/OPOM. 虽然人脸识别技术在日常生活中很方便，但也引起了社交媒体普通用户的隐私问题，因为它们可以用来高效、秘密地分析人脸图像和视频，而没有任何安全限制。在本文中，我们从技术角度研究了基于新型定制斗篷的人脸隐私保护，该斗篷可以应用于普通用户的所有图像，以防止恶意人脸识别系统暴露他们的身份。具体来说，我们提出了一种名为一人一掩码（OPOM）的新方法，通过在远离源身份的特征子空间的方向上优化每个训练样本来生成特定于人的（按类别）通用掩码。为了充分利用有限的训练图像，我们研究了几种建模方法，包括仿射壳、类中心和凸包，以获得对源身份的特征子空间的更好描述。该方法的有效性在常见数据集和名人数据集上针对具有不同损失函数和网络架构的黑盒人脸识别模型进行了评估。此外，我们讨论了所提出方法的优点和潜在问题。特别是，我们对视频数据集 Sherlock 的隐私保护进行了应用研究，以展示所提出方法的潜在实际用途。数据集和代码可在 https://github.com/zhongyy/OPOM 获取。 本文提出一种名为OPOM的个性化面部隐私保护方法，通过生成定制化隐形面具来防止恶意人脸识别系统识别用户身份。 Yaoyao Zhong,Weihong Deng cs.CV

authors_text: Yuan Wang,Huiling Song,Peng Huo,Tao Xu,Jucheng Yang,Yarui Chen,Tingting Zhao
title: Exploiting Dynamic and Fine-grained Semantic Scope for Extreme Multi-label Text Classification
title_zh: 利用动态和细粒度语义范围进行极端多标签文本分类
abstract: Extreme multi-label text classification (XMTC) refers to the problem of tagging a given text with the most relevant subset of labels from a large label set. A majority of labels only have a few training instances due to large label dimensionality in XMTC. To solve this data sparsity issue, most existing XMTC methods take advantage of fixed label clusters obtained in early stage to balance performance on tail labels and head labels. However, such label clusters provide static and coarse-grained semantic scope for every text, which ignores distinct characteristics of different texts and has difficulties modelling accurate semantics scope for texts with tail labels. In this paper, we propose a novel framework TReaderXML for XMTC, which adopts dynamic and fine-grained semantic scope from teacher knowledge for individual text to optimize text conditional prior category semantic ranges. TReaderXML dynamically obtains teacher knowledge for each text by similar texts and hierarchical label information in training sets to release the ability of distinctly fine-grained label-oriented semantic scope. Then, TReaderXML benefits from a novel dual cooperative network that firstly learns features of a text and its corresponding label-oriented semantic scope by parallel Encoding Module and Reading Module, secondly embeds two parts by Interaction Module to regularize the text's representation by dynamic and fine-grained label-oriented semantic scope, and finally find target labels by Prediction Module. Experimental results on three XMTC benchmark datasets show that our method achieves new state-of-the-art results and especially performs well for severely imbalanced and sparse datasets.
summary: Extreme multi-label text classification (XMTC) refers to the problem of tagging a given text with the most relevant subset of labels from a large label set. A majority of labels only have a few training instances due to large label dimensionality in XMTC. To solve this data sparsity issue, most existing XMTC methods take advantage of fixed label clusters obtained in early stage to balance performance on tail labels and head labels. However, such label clusters provide static and coarse-grained semantic scope for every text, which ignores distinct characteristics of different texts and has difficulties modelling accurate semantics scope for texts with tail labels. In this paper, we propose a novel framework TReaderXML for XMTC, which adopts dynamic and fine-grained semantic scope from teacher knowledge for individual text to optimize text conditional prior category semantic ranges. TReaderXML dynamically obtains teacher knowledge for each text by similar texts and hierarchical label information in training sets to release the ability of distinctly fine-grained label-oriented semantic scope. Then, TReaderXML benefits from a novel dual cooperative network that firstly learns features of a text and its corresponding label-oriented semantic scope by parallel Encoding Module and Reading Module, secondly embeds two parts by Interaction Module to regularize the text's representation by dynamic and fine-grained label-oriented semantic scope, and finally find target labels by Prediction Module. Experimental results on three XMTC benchmark datasets show that our method achieves new state-of-the-art results and especially performs well for severely imbalanced and sparse datasets.
abstract_zh: 极端多标签文本分类（XMTC）是指使用大型标签集中最相关的标签子集来标记给定文本的问题。由于 XMTC 中标签维度较大，大多数标签只有少量训练实例。为了解决这个数据稀疏问题，大多数现有的XMTC方法利用早期获得的固定标签簇来平衡尾部标签和头部标签的性能。然而，这种标签簇为每个文本提供静态和粗粒度的语义范围，它忽略了不同文本的独特特征，并且难以为带有尾部标签的文本建模准确的语义范围。在本文中，我们提出了一种用于 XMTC 的新颖框架 TReaderXML，该框架采用来自教师知识的动态且细粒度的语义范围来优化文本条件先验类别语义范围。 TReaderXML通过训练集中的相似文本和分层标签信息动态获取每个文本的教师知识，释放明显细粒度的面向标签的语义范围的能力。然后，TReaderXML受益于一种新颖的双协作网络，首先通过并行编码模块和阅读模块学习文本的特征及其相应的面向标签的语义范围，其次通过交互模块嵌入两个部分，通过动态和细粒度的面向标签的语义范围来规范文本的表示，最后通过预测模块​​找到目标标签。在三个 XMTC 基准​​数据集上的实验结果表明，我们的方法取得了新的最先进的结果，尤其是在严重不平衡和稀疏的数据集上表现良好。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为TReaderXML的框架，用于解决极端多标签文本分类（XMTC）中标签数据稀疏性和语义范围建模不足的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有XMTC方法依赖静态粗粒度标签聚类，无法捕捉文本的动态细粒度语义范围，导致尾部标签性能不佳。
AI.method: 通过教师知识动态获取文本的细粒度语义范围，结合并行编码模块、阅读模块、交互模块和预测模块的双协同网络进行优化。
AI.result: 在三个XMTC基准数据集上达到新SOTA结果，尤其在严重不平衡和稀疏数据集上表现优异。
AI.conclusion: TReaderXML通过动态细粒度语义建模有效解决了XMTC中的数据稀疏性问题，为极端多标签分类提供了新思路。

authors_text: Sanne G. M. van Velzen,Bob D. de Vos,Julia M. H. Noothout,Helena M. Verkooijen,Max A. Viergever,Ivana Išgum
title: Generative Models for Reproducible Coronary Calcium Scoring
title_zh: 可重复的冠状动脉钙评分的生成模型
abstract: Purpose: Coronary artery calcium (CAC) score, i.e. the amount of CAC quantified in CT, is a strong and independent predictor of coronary heart disease (CHD) events. However, CAC scoring suffers from limited interscan reproducibility, which is mainly due to the clinical definition requiring application of a fixed intensity level threshold for segmentation of calcifications. This limitation is especially pronounced in non-ECG-synchronized CT where lesions are more impacted by cardiac motion and partial volume effects. Therefore, we propose a CAC quantification method that does not require a threshold for segmentation of CAC. Approach: Our method utilizes a generative adversarial network where a CT with CAC is decomposed into an image without CAC and an image showing only CAC. The method, using a CycleGAN, was trained using 626 low-dose chest CTs and 514 radiotherapy treatment planning CTs. Interscan reproducibility was compared to clinical calcium scoring in radiotherapy treatment planning CTs of 1,662 patients, each having two scans. Results: A lower relative interscan difference in CAC mass was achieved by the proposed method: 47% compared to 89% manual clinical calcium scoring. The intraclass correlation coefficient of Agatston scores was 0.96 for the proposed method compared to 0.91 for automatic clinical calcium scoring. Conclusions: The increased interscan reproducibility achieved by our method may lead to increased reliability of CHD risk categorization and improved accuracy of CHD event prediction.
summary: Purpose: Coronary artery calcium (CAC) score, i.e. the amount of CAC quantified in CT, is a strong and independent predictor of coronary heart disease (CHD) events. However, CAC scoring suffers from limited interscan reproducibility, which is mainly due to the clinical definition requiring application of a fixed intensity level threshold for segmentation of calcifications. This limitation is especially pronounced in non-ECG-synchronized CT where lesions are more impacted by cardiac motion and partial volume effects. Therefore, we propose a CAC quantification method that does not require a threshold for segmentation of CAC. Approach: Our method utilizes a generative adversarial network where a CT with CAC is decomposed into an image without CAC and an image showing only CAC. The method, using a CycleGAN, was trained using 626 low-dose chest CTs and 514 radiotherapy treatment planning CTs. Interscan reproducibility was compared to clinical calcium scoring in radiotherapy treatment planning CTs of 1,662 patients, each having two scans. Results: A lower relative interscan difference in CAC mass was achieved by the proposed method: 47% compared to 89% manual clinical calcium scoring. The intraclass correlation coefficient of Agatston scores was 0.96 for the proposed method compared to 0.91 for automatic clinical calcium scoring. Conclusions: The increased interscan reproducibility achieved by our method may lead to increased reliability of CHD risk categorization and improved accuracy of CHD event prediction.
abstract_zh: 目的：冠状动脉钙化 (CAC) 评分，即 CT 中量化的 CAC 量，是冠心病 (CHD) 事件的强有力且独立的预测因子。然而，CAC 评分的扫描间再现性有限，这主要是由于临床定义需要应用固定的强度水平阈值来分割钙化。这种限制在非心电图同步 CT 中尤其明显，其中病变更容易受到心脏运动和部分容积效应的影响。因此，我们提出了一种不需要阈值来分割CAC的CAC量化方法。方法：我们的方法利用生成对抗网络，将具有 CAC 的 CT 分解为不带 CAC 的图像和仅显示 CAC 的图像。该方法使用 CycleGAN，并使用 626 个低剂量胸部 CT 和 514 个放射治疗计划 CT 进行训练。将扫描间再现性与 1,662 名患者的放射治疗计划 CT 中的临床钙评分进行比较，每名患者进行两次扫描。结果：通过所提出的方法实现了较低的 CAC 质量相对扫描间差异：与手动临床钙评分的 89% 相比，为 47%。该方法的 Agatston 评分的组内相关系数为 0.96，而自动临床钙评分的组内相关系数为 0.91。结论：通过我们的方法实现的扫描间再现性的提高可能会提高 CHD 风险分类的可靠性并提高 CHD 事件预测的准确性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于生成对抗网络的冠状动脉钙化评分方法，以提高CT扫描间的可重复性。
AI.motivation: 传统冠状动脉钙化评分方法因依赖固定阈值导致扫描间可重复性差，尤其在非ECG同步CT中受心脏运动和部分容积效应影响显著。
AI.method: 采用CycleGAN生成对抗网络，将含钙化CT图像分解为无钙化图像和纯钙化图像，利用626例低剂量胸部CT和514例放疗计划CT进行训练。
AI.result: 新方法钙化质量扫描间相对差异为47%（临床手动评分89%），Agatston评分组内相关系数达0.96（临床自动评分0.91）。
AI.conclusion: 该方法提升的扫描间可重复性有望增强冠心病风险分类可靠性及事件预测准确性。

ai_content.conclusion: 该方法提升的扫描间可重复性有望增强冠心病风险分类可靠性及事件预测准确性。

Conclusions: The increased interscan reproducibility achieved by our method may lead to increased reliability of CHD risk categorization and improved accuracy of CHD event prediction. 目的：冠状动脉钙化 (CAC) 评分，即 CT 中量化的 CAC 量，是冠心病 (CHD) 事件的强有力且独立的预测因子。然而，CAC 评分的扫描间再现性有限，这主要是由于临床定义需要应用固定的强度水平阈值来分割钙化。这种限制在非心电图同步 CT 中尤其明显，其中病变更容易受到心脏运动和部分容积效应的影响。因此，我们提出了一种不需要阈值来分割CAC的CAC量化方法。方法：我们的方法利用生成对抗网络，将具有 CAC 的 CT 分解为不带 CAC 的图像和仅显示 CAC 的图像。该方法使用 CycleGAN，并使用 626 个低剂量胸部 CT 和 514 个放射治疗计划 CT 进行训练。将扫描间再现性与 1,662 名患者的放射治疗计划 CT 中的临床钙评分进行比较，每名患者进行两次扫描。结果：通过所提出的方法实现了较低的 CAC 质量相对扫描间差异：与手动临床钙评分的 89% 相比，为 47%。该方法的 Agatston 评分的组内相关系数为 0.96，而自动临床钙评分的组内相关系数为 0.91。结论：通过我们的方法实现的扫描间再现性的提高可能会提高 CHD 风险分类的可靠性并提高 CHD 事件预测的准确性。 本文提出一种基于生成对抗网络的冠状动脉钙化评分方法，以提高CT扫描间的可重复性。 Sanne G. M. van Velzen,Bob D. de Vos,Julia M. H. Noothout,Helena M. Verkooijen,Max A. Viergever,Ivana Išgum eess.IV,cs.CV

title: The Data-Production Dispositif
title_zh: 数据生产配置
comment: Accepted for publication at CSCW 2022. Forthcoming in the Proceedings of the ACM on Human-Computer Interaction
comments: Accepted for publication at CSCW 2022. Forthcoming in the Proceedings of the ACM on Human-Computer Interaction
abstract: Machine learning (ML) depends on data to train and verify models. Very often, organizations outsource processes related to data work (i.e., generating and annotating data and evaluating outputs) through business process outsourcing (BPO) companies and crowdsourcing platforms. This paper investigates outsourced ML data work in Latin America by studying three platforms in Venezuela and a BPO in Argentina. We lean on the Foucauldian notion of dispositif to define the data-production dispositif as an ensemble of discourses, actions, and objects strategically disposed to (re)produce power/knowledge relations in data and labor. Our dispositif analysis comprises the examination of 210 data work instruction documents, 55 interviews with data workers, managers, and requesters, and participant observation. Our findings show that discourses encoded in instructions reproduce and normalize the worldviews of requesters. Precarious working conditions and economic dependency alienate workers, making them obedient to instructions. Furthermore, discourses and social contexts materialize in artifacts, such as interfaces and performance metrics, limiting workers' agency and normalizing specific ways of interpreting data. We conclude by stressing the importance of counteracting the data-production dispositif by fighting alienation and precarization, and empowering data workers to become assets in the quest for high-quality data.

Our dispositif analysis comprises the examination of 210 data work instruction documents, 55 interviews with data workers, managers, and requesters, and participant observation. Our findings show that discourses encoded in instructions reproduce and normalize the worldviews of requesters. Precarious working conditions and economic dependency alienate workers, making them obedient to instructions. abstract_zh: 机器学习 (ML) 依赖数据来训练和验证模型。通常，组织通过业务流程外包 (BPO) 公司和众包平台外包与数据工作相关的流程（即生成和注释数据以及评估输出）。本文通过研究委内瑞拉的三个平台和阿根廷的 BPO 来调查拉丁美洲的外包 ML 数据工作。我们依靠福柯的配置概念将数据生产配置定义为战略性地用于（重新）生产数据和劳动中的权力/知识关系的话语、行动和对象的集合。我们的处置分析包括对 210 份数据工作指导文件的检查、对数据工作者、管理者和请求者的 55 次访谈以及参与观察。我们的研究结果表明，指令中编码的话语再现并规范了请求者的世界观。不稳定的工作条件和经济依赖使工人疏远，使他们服从指示。此外，话语和社会背景在人工制品中具体化，例如界面和绩效指标，限制了工人的能动性并使解释数据的特定方式标准化。最后，我们强调通过对抗异化和不稳定来抵消数据生产配置的重要性，并授权数据工作者成为寻求高质量数据的资产。

AI.tldr: 本文通过研究拉丁美洲的数据外包平台，分析机器学习数据生产中的权力关系结构。
AI.motivation: 机器学习依赖数据生产，而外包模式可能加剧劳动异化与权力不平等，需揭示其背后的社会机制。
AI.method: 结合福柯的dispositif理论，分析210份指令文档、55次访谈及参与观察，研究数据工作者的劳动条件与话语权力。
AI.result: 发现指令话语强化请求者世界观，不稳定工作条件导致工人顺从，技术界面与指标限制其主体性。
AI.conclusion: 需通过对抗异化与不稳定，赋予数据工作者更多权利，以提升数据质量与劳动权益。

title: A Wireless-Vision Dataset for Privacy Preserving Human Activity Recognition
title_zh: 用于保护隐私的人类活动识别的无线视觉数据集
abstract: Human Activity Recognition (HAR) has recently received remarkable attention in numerous applications such as assisted living and remote monitoring. Existing solutions based on sensors and vision technologies have obtained achievements but still suffering from considerable limitations in the environmental requirement. Wireless signals like WiFi-based sensing have emerged as a new paradigm since it is convenient and not restricted in the environment. In this paper, a new WiFi-based and video-based neural network (WiNN) is proposed to improve the robustness of activity recognition where the synchronized video serves as the supplement for the wireless data. Moreover, a wireless-vision benchmark (WiVi) is collected for 9 class actions recognition in three different visual conditions, including the scenes without occlusion, with partial occlusion, and with full occlusion. Both machine learning methods - support vector machine (SVM) as well as deep learning methods are used for the accuracy verification of the data set. Our results show that WiVi data set satisfies the primary demand and all three branches in the proposed pipeline keep more than $80\%$ of activity recognition accuracy over multiple action segmentation from 1s to 3s. In particular, WiNN is the most robust method in terms of all the actions on three action segmentation compared to the others.
summary: Human Activity Recognition (HAR) has recently received remarkable attention in numerous applications such as assisted living and remote monitoring. Existing solutions based on sensors and vision technologies have obtained achievements but still suffering from considerable limitations in the environmental requirement. Wireless signals like WiFi-based sensing have emerged as a new paradigm since it is convenient and not restricted in the environment. In this paper, a new WiFi-based and video-based neural network (WiNN) is proposed to improve the robustness of activity recognition where the synchronized video serves as the supplement for the wireless data. Moreover, a wireless-vision benchmark (WiVi) is collected for 9 class actions recognition in three different visual conditions, including the scenes without occlusion, with partial occlusion, and with full occlusion. Both machine learning methods - support vector machine (SVM) as well as deep learning methods are used for the accuracy verification of the data set. Our results show that WiVi data set satisfies the primary demand and all three branches in the proposed pipeline keep more than $80\%$ of activity recognition accuracy over multiple action segmentation from 1s to 3s. In particular, WiNN is the most robust method in terms of all the actions on three action segmentation compared to the others.
abstract_zh: 人类活动识别（HAR）最近在辅助生活和远程监控等众多应用中受到了广泛关注。现有的基于传感器和视觉技术的解决方案已经取得了一定的成就，但在环境要求方面仍然受到相当大的限制。基于 WiFi 的传感等无线信号已经成为一种新的范例，因为它方便且不受环境限制。本文提出了一种新的基于 WiFi 和视频的神经网络（WiNN），以提高活动识别的鲁棒性，其中同步视频作为无线数据的补充。此外，还收集了无线视觉基准（WiVi），用于三种不同视觉条件下的 9 类动作识别，包括无遮挡、部分遮挡和完全遮挡的场景。机器学习方法——支持向量机（SVM）以及深度学习方法都用于数据集的准确性验证。我们的结果表明，WiVi 数据集满足主要需求，并且拟议管道中的所有三个分支在 1 秒到 3 秒的多个动作分段中保持超过 80\%$ 的活动识别精度。特别是，与其他方法相比，就三个动作分割上的所有动作而言，WiNN 是最稳健的方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合WiFi信号与视频数据的神经网络模型（WiNN）及无线-视觉基准数据集（WiVi），用于隐私保护的人类活动识别。
AI.motivation: 现有传感器和视觉技术受环境限制，而WiFi信号传感更具环境适应性，但需视觉数据补充以提升鲁棒性，同时需解决隐私问题。
AI.method: 设计同步WiFi信号与视频数据的WiNN模型，构建包含无遮挡、部分遮挡和全遮挡三种场景的WiVi数据集，采用SVM与深度学习方法验证性能。
AI.result: WiVi数据集满足需求，三种动作分割场景下识别准确率均超80%，其中WiNN模型在所有动作上表现最稳健。
AI.conclusion: WiVi数据集与WiNN方法有效提升了隐私保护场景下活动识别的鲁棒性，为未来研究提供了新方向。

title: 3D helical CT Reconstruction with a Memory Efficient Learned Primal-Dual Architecture
title_zh: 使用内存高效的学习原始-对偶架构进行 3D 螺旋 CT 重建
abstract: Deep learning based computed tomography (CT) reconstruction has demonstrated outstanding performance on simulated 2D low-dose CT data. This applies in particular to domain adapted neural networks, which incorporate a handcrafted physics model for CT imaging. Empirical evidence shows that employing such architectures reduces the demand for training data and improves upon generalisation. However, their training requires large computational resources that quickly become prohibitive in 3D helical CT, which is the most common acquisition geometry used for medical imaging. Furthermore, clinical data also comes with other challenges not accounted for in simulations, like errors in flux measurement, resolution mismatch and, most importantly, the absence of the real ground truth. The necessity to have a computationally feasible training combined with the need to address these issues has made it difficult to evaluate deep learning based reconstruction on clinical 3D helical CT. This paper modifies a domain adapted neural network architecture, the Learned Primal-Dual (LPD), so that it can be trained and applied to reconstruction in this setting. We achieve this by splitting the helical trajectory into sections and applying the unrolled LPD iterations to those sections sequentially. To the best of our knowledge, this work is the first to apply an unrolled deep learning architecture for reconstruction on full-sized clinical data, like those in the Low dose CT image and projection data set (LDCT). Moreover, training and testing is done on a single GPU card with 24GB of memory.
summary: Deep learning based computed tomography (CT) reconstruction has demonstrated outstanding performance on simulated 2D low-dose CT data. This applies in particular to domain adapted neural networks, which incorporate a handcrafted physics model for CT imaging. Empirical evidence shows that employing such architectures reduces the demand for training data and improves upon generalisation. However, their training requires large computational resources that quickly become prohibitive in 3D helical CT, which is the most common acquisition geometry used for medical imaging. Furthermore, clinical data also comes with other challenges not accounted for in simulations, like errors in flux measurement, resolution mismatch and, most importantly, the absence of the real ground truth. The necessity to have a computationally feasible training combined with the need to address these issues has made it difficult to evaluate deep learning based reconstruction on clinical 3D helical CT. This paper modifies a domain adapted neural network architecture, the Learned Primal-Dual (LPD), so that it can be trained and applied to reconstruction in this setting. We achieve this by splitting the helical trajectory into sections and applying the unrolled LPD iterations to those sections sequentially. To the best of our knowledge, this work is the first to apply an unrolled deep learning architecture for reconstruction on full-sized clinical data, like those in the Low dose CT image and projection data set (LDCT). Moreover, training and testing is done on a single GPU card with 24GB of memory.
abstract_zh: 基于深度学习的计算机断层扫描 (CT) 重建在模拟 2D 低剂量 CT 数据上表现出了出色的性能。这尤其适用于领域适应神经网络，它结合了用于 CT 成像的手工物理模型。经验证据表明，采用这种架构可以减少对训练数据的需求并提高泛化能力。 However, their training requires large computational resources that quickly become prohibitive in 3D helical CT, which is the most common acquisition geometry used for medical imaging. Furthermore, clinical data also comes with other challenges not accounted for in simulations, like errors in flux measurement, resolution mismatch and, most importantly, the absence of the real ground truth. The necessity to have a computationally feasible training combined with the need to address these issues has made it difficult to evaluate deep learning based reconstruction on clinical 3D helical CT. This paper modifies a domain adapted neural network architecture, the Learned Primal-Dual (LPD), so that it can be trained and applied to reconstruction in this setting.我们通过将螺旋轨迹分成几个部分并按顺序将展开的 LPD 迭代应用于这些部分来实现这一点。 To the best of our knowledge, this work is the first to apply an unrolled deep learning architecture for reconstruction on full-sized clinical data, like those in the Low dose CT image and projection data set (LDCT).此外，训练和测试是在具有 24GB 内存的单个 GPU 卡上完成的。

AI.tldr: 提出一种内存高效的深度学习方法，用于3D螺旋CT图像重建。
AI.motivation: 传统深度学习方法在3D螺旋CT中面临计算资源限制和临床数据挑战，需改进以实现高效训练和重建。
AI.method: 将螺旋轨迹分段处理，使用展开的LPD网络进行分段重建，并在单GPU上训练。
AI.result: 首次在完整临床数据（如LDCT数据集）上应用展开深度学习架构，实现单GPU训练与测试。
AI.conclusion: 改进后的LPD架构有效降低了内存需求，使深度学习在3D螺旋CT重建中可行。

search_text: 3D helical CT Reconstruction with a Memory Efficient Learned Primal-Dual Architecture 使用内存高效的学习原始-对偶架构进行 3D 螺旋 CT 重建 Deep learning based computed tomography (CT) reconstruction has demonstrated outstanding performance on simulated 2D low-dose CT data. This applies in particular to domain adapted neural networks, which incorporate a handcrafted physics model for CT imaging. Empirical evidence shows that employing such architectures reduces the demand for training data and improves upon generalisation. However, their training requires large computational resources that quickly become prohibitive in 3D helical CT, which is the most common acquisition geometry used for medical imaging. Furthermore, clinical data also comes with other challenges not accounted for in simulations, like errors in flux measurement, resolution mismatch and, most importantly, the absence of the real ground truth. The necessity to have a computationally feasible training combined with the need to address these issues has made it difficult to evaluate deep learning based reconstruction on clinical 3D helical CT. This paper modifies a domain adapted neural network architecture, the Learned Primal-Dual (LPD), so that it can be trained and applied to reconstruction in this setting. We achieve this by splitting the helical trajectory into sections and applying the unrolled LPD iterations to those sections sequentially. To the best of our knowledge, this work is the first to apply an unrolled deep learning architecture for reconstruction on full-sized clinical data, like those in the Low dose CT image and projection data set (LDCT). Moreover, training and testing is done on a single GPU card with 24GB of memory. 基于深度学习的计算机断层扫描 (CT) 重建在模拟 2D 低剂量 CT 数据上表现出了出色的性能。这尤其适用于领域适应神经网络，它结合了用于 CT 成像的手工物理模型。经验证据表明，采用这种架构可以减少对训练数据的需求并提高泛化能力。 However, their training requires large computational resources that quickly become prohibitive in 3D helical CT, which is the most common acquisition geometry used for medical imaging. Furthermore, clinical data also comes with other challenges not accounted for in simulations, like errors in flux measurement, resolution mismatch and, most importantly, the absence of the real ground truth. The necessity to have a computationally feasible training combined with the need to address these issues has made it difficult to evaluate deep learning based reconstruction on clinical 3D helical CT. title: Diffuse Map Guiding Unsupervised Generative Adversarial Network for SVBRDF Estimation
title_zh: 用于 SVBRDF 估计的漫射地图引导无监督生成对抗网络
abstract: Reconstructing materials in the real world has always been a difficult problem in computer graphics. Accurately reconstructing the material in the real world is critical in the field of realistic rendering. Traditionally, materials in computer graphics are mapped by an artist, then mapped onto a geometric model by coordinate transformation, and finally rendered with a rendering engine to get realistic materials. For opaque objects, the industry commonly uses physical-based bidirectional reflectance distribution function (BRDF) rendering models for material modeling. The commonly used physical-based rendering models are Cook-Torrance BRDF, Disney BRDF. In this paper, we use the Cook-Torrance model to reconstruct the materials. The SVBRDF material parameters include Normal, Diffuse, Specular and Roughness. This paper presents a Diffuse map guiding material estimation method based on the Generative Adversarial Network(GAN). This method can predict plausible SVBRDF maps with global features using only a few pictures taken by the mobile phone. The main contributions of this paper are: 1) We preprocess a small number of input pictures to produce a large number of non-repeating pictures for training to reduce over-fitting. 2) We use a novel method to directly obtain the guessed diffuse map with global characteristics, which provides more prior information for the training process. 3) We improve the network architecture of the generator so that it can generate fine details of normal maps and reduce the possibility to generate over-flat normal maps. The method used in this paper can obtain prior knowledge without using dataset training, which greatly reduces the difficulty of material reconstruction and saves a lot of time to generate and calibrate datasets.
summary: Reconstructing materials in the real world has always been a difficult problem in computer graphics. Accurately reconstructing the material in the real world is critical in the field of realistic rendering. Traditionally, materials in computer graphics are mapped by an artist, then mapped onto a geometric model by coordinate transformation, and finally rendered with a rendering engine to get realistic materials. For opaque objects, the industry commonly uses physical-based bidirectional reflectance distribution function (BRDF) rendering models for material modeling. The commonly used physical-based rendering models are Cook-Torrance BRDF, Disney BRDF. In this paper, we use the Cook-Torrance model to reconstruct the materials. The SVBRDF material parameters include Normal, Diffuse, Specular and Roughness. This paper presents a Diffuse map guiding material estimation method based on the Generative Adversarial Network(GAN). This method can predict plausible SVBRDF maps with global features using only a few pictures taken by the mobile phone. The main contributions of this paper are: 1) We preprocess a small number of input pictures to produce a large number of non-repeating pictures for training to reduce over-fitting. 2) We use a novel method to directly obtain the guessed diffuse map with global characteristics, which provides more prior information for the training process. 3) We improve the network architecture of the generator so that it can generate fine details of normal maps and reduce the possibility to generate over-flat normal maps. The method used in this paper can obtain prior knowledge without using dataset training, which greatly reduces the difficulty of material reconstruction and saves a lot of time to generate and calibrate datasets.
abstract_zh: 重建现实世界中的材质一直是计算机图形学中的一个难题。准确地重建现实世界中的材质在写实渲染领域至关重要。传统上，计算机图形学中的材质是由美术师绘制的，然后通过坐标变换映射到几何模型上，最后使用渲染引擎进行渲染以获得逼真的材质。对于不透明物体，业界普遍采用基于物理的双向反射分布函数（BRDF）渲染模型进行材质建模。常用的基于物理的渲染模型有Cook-Torrance BRDF、Disney BRDF。在本文中，我们使用 Cook-Torrance 模型来重建材料。 SVBRDF 材质参数包括法线、漫反射、镜面反射和粗糙度。提出一种基于生成对抗网络（GAN）的漫反射图引导材料估计方法。该方法可以仅使用手机拍摄的几张照片来预测具有全局特征的合理 SVBRDF 地图。本文的主要贡献是：1）我们对少量输入图片进行预处理，产生大量非重复图片用于训练，以减少过拟合。 2）我们使用一种新颖的方法直接获得具有全局特征的猜测漫反射图，这为训练过程提供了更多的先验信息。 3）我们改进了生成器的网络架构，使其能够生成法线贴图的精细细节，并减少生成过平坦法线贴图的可能性。本文采用的方法无需使用数据集训练即可获得先验知识，大大降低了材料重构的难度，节省了大量生成和校准数据集的时间。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于生成对抗网络（GAN）的扩散图引导方法，用于从少量手机拍摄图像中估计表面各向异性反射率分布函数（SVBRDF）参数。
AI.motivation: 传统材质重建依赖人工映射和复杂渲染流程，耗时且难以处理真实场景。现有物理渲染模型需大量标注数据，而本文旨在通过无监督学习降低数据准备难度。
AI.method: 1) 通过图像预处理生成非重复训练样本；2) 利用扩散图提供全局先验信息；3) 改进生成器网络结构以提升法线图细节生成能力。
AI.result: 方法能在少量输入图像下生成合理SVBRDF地图，有效减少过拟合，生成更精细的法线图细节，无需依赖大规模数据集训练。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过扩散图引导的GAN架构显著降低了材质重建的复杂度和数据准备成本，为真实场景材质估计提供了高效解决方案。

authors_text: Sai Sagar Jinka,Astitva Srivastava,Chandradeep Pokhariya,Avinash Sharma,P. J. Narayanan
title: SHARP: Shape-Aware Reconstruction of People in Loose Clothing
title_zh: SHARP：对穿着宽松衣服的人进行形状感知重建
abstract: Recent advancements in deep learning have enabled 3D human body reconstruction from a monocular image, which has broad applications in multiple domains. In this paper, we propose SHARP (SHape Aware Reconstruction of People in loose clothing), a novel end-to-end trainable network that accurately recovers the 3D geometry and appearance of humans in loose clothing from a monocular image. SHARP uses a sparse and efficient fusion strategy to combine parametric body prior with a non-parametric 2D representation of clothed humans. The parametric body prior enforces geometrical consistency on the body shape and pose, while the non-parametric representation models loose clothing and handle self-occlusions as well. We also leverage the sparseness of the non-parametric representation for faster training of our network while using losses on 2D maps. Another key contribution is 3DHumans, our new life-like dataset of 3D human body scans with rich geometrical and textural details. We evaluate SHARP on 3DHumans and other publicly available datasets and show superior qualitative and quantitative performance than existing state-of-the-art methods.
summary: Recent advancements in deep learning have enabled 3D human body reconstruction from a monocular image, which has broad applications in multiple domains. In this paper, we propose SHARP (SHape Aware Reconstruction of People in loose clothing), a novel end-to-end trainable network that accurately recovers the 3D geometry and appearance of humans in loose clothing from a monocular image. SHARP uses a sparse and efficient fusion strategy to combine parametric body prior with a non-parametric 2D representation of clothed humans. The parametric body prior enforces geometrical consistency on the body shape and pose, while the non-parametric representation models loose clothing and handle self-occlusions as well. We also leverage the sparseness of the non-parametric representation for faster training of our network while using losses on 2D maps. Another key contribution is 3DHumans, our new life-like dataset of 3D human body scans with rich geometrical and textural details. We evaluate SHARP on 3DHumans and other publicly available datasets and show superior qualitative and quantitative performance than existing state-of-the-art methods.
abstract_zh: 深度学习的最新进展使得能够从单目图像重建 3D 人体，这在多个领域具有广泛的应用。在本文中，我们提出了 SHARP（宽松衣服人物形状感知重建），这是一种新颖的端到端可训练网络，可以从单目图像中准确恢复穿着宽松衣服的人的 3D 几何形状和外观。 SHARP 使用稀疏且高效的融合策略将参数化身体先验与穿着人体的非参数化 2D 表示相结合。参数化身体先验强制身体形状和姿势的几何一致性，而非参数化表示则对宽松的衣服进行建模并处理自遮挡。我们还利用非参数表示的稀疏性来更快地训练我们的网络，同时在 2D 地图上使用损失。另一个关键贡献是 3DHumans，这是我们新的逼真的 3D 人体扫描数据集，具有丰富的几何和纹理细节。我们在 3DHumans 和其他公开可用的数据集上评估 SHARP，并显示出比现有最先进方法更优越的定性和定量性能。

AI.tldr: 提出SHARP方法，从单目图像中精确重建穿宽松衣物的人体3D几何与外观
AI.motivation: 现有方法在处理宽松衣物及自遮挡时存在挑战，需更高效准确的重建方法
AI.method: 融合参数化身体先验与非参数2D表示，利用稀疏性加速训练，并采用2D地图损失函数
AI.result: 在3DHumans等数据集上实现更优的定性和定量重建效果，优于现有SOTA方法
AI.conclusion: 提出SHARP方法及3DHumans数据集，推动单目图像下人体3D重建研究
